game_name: Zork I: The Great Underground Empire

danger_signals.visual_form: textual description of dark areas without light source
danger_signals.meaning: imminent danger of being eaten by a grue; player must carry lantern or light source to survive

danger_signals.visual_form: textual notification of thief wandering the underground, stealing items from player inventory or found locations
danger_signals.meaning: active threat that steals possessions; player can engage in combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: textual description of creatures or obstacles in specific locations
danger_signals.meaning: locations contain hostile entities requiring combat or avoidance to overcome

danger_signals.visual_form: textual description of impassable locations, dams, mazes, or environmental hazards (e.g., 'Loud Room')
danger_signals.meaning: world-state blocking progression; requires problem-solving to proceed safely

safety_signals.visual_form: textual notification of obtaining lamp or other light-emitting object
safety_signals.meaning: player now safe from grue in dark areas; enables exploration of previously dangerous zones

safety_signals.visual_form: textual description of environmental change after solving a puzzle (e.g., dam emptied, maze traversed)
safety_signals.meaning: passageway now clear; world-state has shifted to allow progression

safety_signals.visual_form: textual notification of score increment when treasure collected or task accomplished
safety_signals.meaning: progress marker indicating player is advancing through game objectives safely

safety_signals.visual_form: textual confirmation of successful combat with thief or successful evasion
safety_signals.meaning: threat neutralized; player inventory now secure

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: inventory system, score tracking, treasure collection

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: wandering antagonist with independent AI behavior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: thief's treasure room (location where stolen items accumulate), active theft notifications

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: creature exclusive to dark areas; eats player if light source absent

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: ruins, abandoned structures, scattered treasures, puzzle mechanisms (dams, buttons), maze systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: textual descriptions become minimal or warnings; imminent danger text
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player character gets eaten by grue; game over state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: full location descriptions available; normal exploration text
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can safely navigate, interact with objects, solve puzzles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: textual description of overwhelming noise in specific location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player cannot perform actions in Loud Room; must solve dam puzzle first

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: textual notification of environmental change; Loud Room now passable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can enter and act in Loud Room without sound interference

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: notifications of thief stealing from locations or player inventory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: items disappear from accessible locations; player must fight thief to recover goods

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: textual notification when player attempts to carry more
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player cannot pick up additional items until weight reduced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: score increment notification displayed as textual feedback
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: progress toward game completion tracked; measurement of world state advancement

environmental_storytelling.example: The Great Underground Empire setting itself communicates historical decline; player explores abandoned structures and scattered treasures indicating civilization collapse
Presence of dams, buttons, mazes, and mechanical systems indicates intentional construction by prior civilization; puzzle design reflects empire's engineering sophistication
Distinctive location names (e.g., 'Loud Room') and textual descriptions convey thematic atmosphere and hint at puzzle mechanics or dangers
Grue exclusive to darkness suggests evolutionary adaptation; thief presence in underground indicates post-collapse scavenging culture
Hidden treasures scattered throughout world communicate valuable remnants of empire; their locations within puzzles suggest previous civilization's protection mechanisms

entity_behavioral_tells.state: player in dark area without light
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: attacks and kills player; game-ending threat with deterministic outcome
steals items from locations and player inventory; moves independently through world; can be fought or evaded
items recovered from thief's treasure room; threat neutralized
can pick up objects; can interact with puzzles and environment
cannot pick up additional items; must drop or use existing inventory
require combat or avoidance to overcome; block progression until defeated or avoided

notes: Zork I is a text-based adventure game, so all world-state communication occurs through textual description and narrative feedback rather than visual graphics. The game employs a text-parsing system where the narrator describes locations, dangers, and consequences of player actions. Danger is primarily communicated through narrative tension (grue threat in darkness, thief stealing items) and puzzle barriers that block progression. Safety is signaled through successful completion states and score increases. Factions are minimal; the primary antagonism is between the player and environmental threats (grue, thief, antagonists). The Great Underground Empire itself is the dominant environmental storytelling device, with ruins and puzzles communicating a collapsed civilization. World-state changes are explicit—the narrator directly announces when conditions change (dam broken, thief defeated, score increased).
